title: Laminate comprising abrasion resisting layer, device comprising the same and method of manufacturing the same
abstract: Disclosed is a laminate (100) comprising an abrasion resisting layer (101) disposed on a surface (102) of an optically transmissive substrate (103), wherein the layer comprises a pattern of spatially separated protrusions (104) of a material extending away from the surface, and silica (105) interposed between the protrusions, wherein the material has a Knoop hardness measured according to ASTM E384 Knoop Hardness Standard which is greater than that of the silica. Further disclosed is a device comprising an optically transmissive component comprising the laminate, and a method of manufacturing the laminate.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A laminate (100) comprising an abrasion resisting layer (101) disposed on a surface (102) of an optically transmissive substrate (103), wherein the layer comprises a pattern of spatially separated protrusions (104) of a material extending away from the surface, and silica (105) interposed between the protrusions, wherein the material has a Knoop hardness measured according to ASTM E384 Knoop Hardness Standard which is greater than that of the silica. 2. The laminate (100) of claim 1, wherein the protrusions (104) comprise extremities (106) which align with an exposed surface (107) of the silica (105) opposing the surface (102), or protrude from the exposed surface. 3. The laminate (100) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the protrusions (104) taper in a direction away from the surface (102). 4. The laminate (100) of claim 3, wherein the protrusions (104) are truncated, and optionally wherein the tapering is such that a smallest width of the protrusion is between 5% and 60% of a largest width of the protrusion. 5. The laminate (100) of any of claims 1-3, wherein the protrusions (104) comprise at least one of a cone-shape, a truncated cone shape, a hyperboloid shape, a pyramid shape, a truncated pyramid shape, a dome shape, and a truncated dome shape. 6. The laminate (100) of any of claims 1-5, wherein the material comprises one or more selected from sapphire, silicon nitride, cubic boron nitride, and diamond. 7. The laminate (100) of any of claims 1-6, wherein the substrate (103) comprises sapphire or diamond. 8. The laminate (100) of any of claims 1-7, wherein a period of the pattern ranges from 10 to 350 nm, and/or a height of the protrusions (104) ranges from 50 to 500 nm. 9. The laminate (100) of any of claims 1-8, wherein the layer (101) comprises a first region (108A) and a second region (108B); the first region having a first pattern and the second region having a second pattern which is different from the first pattern such that the first region and the second region have different refractive indices relative to each other and/or have different optical interference properties relative to each other. 10. The laminate (100) of claim 9, wherein the first pattern and the second pattern comprise differently shaped protrusions (104) relative to each other. 1 1. A device comprising an optically transmissive component comprising the laminate (100) of any of claims 1-10, optionally wherein the optically transmissive component is a window pane or a screen. 12. A method of manufacturing a laminate (100) comprising an abrasion resisting layer (101) disposed on a surface (102) of an optically transmissive substrate (103), the method comprising: providing a layer (20) of a material on the surface (102); structuring the layer to form a pattern of spatially separated protrusions (104) extending away from the surface; applying silica (105) to the pattern such that the silica is interposed between the protrusions; wherein the material has a Knoop hardness measured according to ASTM E384 Knoop Hardness Standard which is greater than that of the silica. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the structuring comprises: providing a patterned etch mask (50) on the layer (20), the mask being patterned such that it covers portions of the layer and leaves further portions uncovered; and selectively etching the layer using the patterned etch mask such that the further portions are at least partially removed, thereby forming the pattern. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the providing a patterned etch mask (50) comprises: providing a pattern precursor layer (30) on the layer (20); imprinting the pattern precursor layer with a relief pattern of a patterned stamp (40); developing the pattern precursor layer into the patterned etch mask; and removing the patterned stamp from the patterned etch mask. 15. The method of any of claims 12-14, wherein the applying comprises: depositing the silica (105) such that it immerses the pattern; partially removing the silica such that extremities (106) of the protrusions (104) align with an exposed surface (107) of the silica opposing the surface (102), or protrude from the exposed surface. 16. The method of claim 14 wherein the patterned stamp comprises a flexible material having the relief pattern.
